team_context.cap_situation: dire — ~$144M in dead cap (Tua $67.4M, Hill $28.2M, Ramsey $20.8M, Armstead $10.7M, Chubb), approximately $10M under cap after restructures. Record dead money in NFL history. 2027 projected ~$91-97M effective cap space.
team_context.trade_posture: Full rebuild under new GM Jon-Eric Sullivan and HC Jeff Hafley (both from Green Bay). Willing to listen on nearly all players. Core building blocks are Waddle, Achane, Brewer, Brooks, and Patrick Paul. Team will build through draft and cheap free agency in 2026, with 2027 as the target for aggressive spending.

players.name: Malik Willis
players.performance_evidence: In 2 seasons with Packers appeared in 11 games with 3 starts (2-1), completing 78.7% of passes for 972 yards and 6 TDs with 0 INTs. Dual-threat ability but very small sample of NFL starter reps. Largely unproven but has high upside athleticism.

trade_value.reasoning: Unproven starter signed to a team-friendly deal for 2026 ($8.7M cap hit) but $45M guaranteed over 2 years makes him expensive to acquire. Essentially untradeable because Miami just signed him to be their bridge/franchise QB. Worth a late 3rd at best based on actual production.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed 3-year, $67.5M deal to be the Dolphins' starting QB. Sullivan and Hafley hand-picked him from their time together in Green Bay. Zero chance of trade.

players.name: Quinn Ewers
players.performance_evidence: Started final 3 games of 2025 season after Tua was benched. 7th-round pick from Texas. Showed enough flashes to earn the backup role. Former #1 overall HS recruit with arm talent but inconsistent reads.

trade_value.reasoning: Young backup QB on a minimum-level deal with intriguing pedigree. Essentially no trade value as a 7th-round pick with 3 career starts, but the cost-controlled deal and youth make him worth holding.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth QB who competes for backup reps behind Willis. Would be available for a mid-to-late-round pick, but minimal demand given his limited resume.

players.name: De'Von Achane
players.performance_evidence: First Pro Bowl selection in 2025. Led NFL in yards per carry (5.7). Rushed for 1,350 yards and 8 TDs on 238 carries, added 67 catches for 488 yards and 4 TDs. Career 7.8 YPC as a rookie, has been explosive every season. Elite speed and dual-threat ability.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl RB on a final-year rookie deal at just $6M cap hit. Elite production but RB position value limits ceiling. Dolphins asked for 2nd-round pick and more at 2025 deadline. Extension-eligible — team views him as building block and is expected to extend him this summer.

players.availability_reasoning: GM Sullivan said he will listen to calls but is not actively trying to move Achane. Dolphins received significant trade interest at 2025 deadline but held firm on a 2nd+ asking price. Team plans to extend him this summer. Would take an overpay offer to pry him loose.
players.injury_notes: Slight frame (5-9, 191) has led to multiple minor injuries each season (knee IR as rookie, ankle/concussion 2024, calf/ribs 2025) but has played every game in last 2 seasons.
players.trade_buzz: ESPN gave trade odds at 10%. Rapoport reported Dolphins received significant trade interest at 2025 deadline. Texans, Chiefs, and others have been linked. Dolphins settled on a 2nd-round pick and more as the price.

players.name: Jaylen Wright
players.performance_evidence: 4th-round pick from Tennessee. Backup RB with blazing speed (7.4 YPC in college). Limited role behind Achane in 2024-2025 as change-of-pace back. Hasn't had significant feature-back opportunity yet.

trade_value.reasoning: Young developmental RB on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years of control. Athletic upside but hasn't carved out a consistent role. Worth a 6th-round pick at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth RB behind Achane. If Miami extends Achane, Wright becomes a depth luxury they could move for a late-round pick if a team comes calling.

players.name: Jaylen Waddle
players.performance_evidence: 64 receptions for 910 yards and 6 TDs in 2025 (16 games). Had 3 consecutive 1,000-yard seasons (2021-2023) before extension. 5,039 career receiving yards. Still explosive and creates elite separation. Hasn't hit 1,000 yards since signing extension but production dipped partly due to QB play and Tyreek Hill injury.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old WR1 with 3 years of team-friendly control ($21M cap hit in 2026 is reasonable). Dolphins asked for a 1st-round pick at 2025 deadline and turned down Buffalo's offer. Contract ramps to $27.7M and $31.1M in later years but still manageable for a contender. Worth a late 1st.

players.availability_reasoning: Sullivan described Waddle as a building block. ESPN gave trade odds at just 10%. Bills offered a 1st-round pick at 2025 deadline and were turned down. Waddle himself has pushed back on trade rumors. However, if a team offers a 1st and more, Miami would have to consider it given the rebuild. Price is a 1st-round pick minimum.
players.injury_notes: Has dealt with minor injuries throughout career but has never made fewer than 14 starts in a season. Durable overall.
players.trade_buzz: Bills tried to acquire him for a 1st-round pick at 2025 deadline. Giants, Broncos, Seahawks, Steelers all called. Dolphins shut down all inquiries. 247Sports' Butler reported Miami receiving trade interest but shutting it down. Sullivan says he'll listen but isn't actively trying to move Waddle.

players.name: Malik Washington
players.performance_evidence: 6th-round pick from Virginia. Moved into a larger role after Tyreek Hill's season-ending injury in Week 4 of 2025. Slot receiver with return ability. Limited production — depth WR elevated by attrition rather than dominance.

trade_value.reasoning: Late-round pick on a minimum deal. Elevated to WR2 by default after Hill injury and roster turnover. Minimal independent trade value — essentially a roster filler.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth WR who could be replaced easily. Would move for any pick from a 6th-round on up.

players.name: Theo Wease Jr.
players.performance_evidence: UDFA from Oklahoma/Missouri. Has bounced around practice squads. WR3 by default on a thin receiver corps following Tyreek Hill's release and roster teardown. Limited NFL production.

trade_value.reasoning: Replacement-level WR on a minimum deal. No meaningful trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Roster filler on a rebuilding team with minimal WR depth. Would be replaced if Miami drafts or signs receivers.

players.name: Greg Dulcich
players.performance_evidence: Caught 26 passes for 335 yards and 1 TD in 10 games with Miami in 2025 after being promoted from practice squad. Led all TEs in avg YAC (7.6) and yards per target (10.2). Former 3rd-round pick by Broncos who flashed as a rookie in 2022 but injuries derailed him. Just re-signed on 1-year, $3.25M deal.

trade_value.reasoning: Low-cost TE1 on a 1-year prove-it deal. Showed promise but hasn't played more than 10 games in any NFL season. Minimal trade value — worth a conditional 7th at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed on a 1-year deal. Miami views him as their starter but would part for any reasonable draft pick given the rebuild.
players.injury_notes: Significant injury history — missed extended time in 2023 and 2024 with various ailments. Has never played more than 10 games in an NFL season.

players.name: Julian Hill
players.performance_evidence: Blocking tight end with minimal receiving production. Was not tendered as an RFA and reportedly signed with Patriots in free agency — may not actually be on MIA roster. Depth piece.

trade_value.reasoning: Replacement-level blocking TE. No trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth TE who may have already signed elsewhere. No real value to hold.
players.trade_buzz: Reports indicate he signed with the Patriots as an unrestricted free agent.

players.name: Patrick Paul
players.performance_evidence: Took over as starting LT after Terron Armstead's departure. 2nd-round pick from Houston (55th overall). Named a building block by GM Sullivan. 6-7, 315 lbs with excellent length and athleticism. Started most of 2025 season. Still developing but shows starter-caliber traits.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cost-controlled starting LT with 3 years remaining on a rookie deal under $2M/year. OT position premium multiplied by youth and team control. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd to a tackle-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Named as a building block by GM Sullivan. Young starting LT on a rookie deal is exactly the kind of player you build around in a rebuild. Miami would not trade him.

players.name: Jonah Savaiinaea
players.performance_evidence: 2nd-round pick from Arizona (37th overall, traded up). Fully guaranteed 4-year deal worth $11.3M. Started as rookie in 2025 but had a 'dismal' rookie campaign per beat reporters. Versatile — can play tackle in a pinch. 6-4, 324 lbs, extremely physical. High ceiling but needs development.

trade_value.reasoning: Young 2nd-round pick on a fully guaranteed rookie deal with 4 years of control. Despite a rough rookie year, his draft pedigree, age, and cost-controlled deal maintain value around a mid-3rd-round pick equivalent.

players.availability_reasoning: Miami traded up to get him and he's only one year into his rookie deal. The new regime didn't draft him, but at 23 on a cheap deal, he's still a long-term investment. Would take an overpay to move him.

players.name: Aaron Brewer
players.performance_evidence: Second-team All-Pro in 2025. Started all 16 games at center. Best offensive lineman on the team. Anchored rushing attack that averaged 4.7 YPC (4th best in NFL in 2024). Named a building block by GM Sullivan. 77.8+ PFF grade. Elite run blocker who also excels in pass protection.

trade_value.reasoning: All-Pro center on a final contract year at $9.15M cap hit. Age (28) and 1 year remaining cap value, but All-Pro production and IOL scarcity boost it. Extension talks expected this summer. Worth a late 2nd if a contender needs a center.

players.availability_reasoning: GM Sullivan named Brewer an extension candidate. Team wants to lock him up long-term. However, entering final year of deal with no guarantees remaining — if extension talks stall, could become available. Dolphins view him as a core piece of the rebuild.
players.injury_notes: Played 916 snaps in 16 games in 2025. Durable throughout career.
players.character_notes: Team captain. Described as tone-setter and leader by coaches and beat reporters.
players.trade_buzz: Dolphins expected to work on extension this summer per Dan Graziano/ESPN.

players.name: Kion Smith
players.performance_evidence: UDFA signed to a reserve/future contract in January 2026. Unproven at the NFL level. Likely competing for starting RG job in camp given the departures of James Daniels and Cole Strange.

trade_value.reasoning: UDFA with no NFL game experience. No trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Camp body who may or may not win the starting RG job. Available for anything.

players.name: Austin Jackson
players.performance_evidence: Former 1st-round pick (18th overall). Has started 60 of 62 games for Dolphins. Just restructured contract to 1-year, $7M prove-it deal with void years, creating $8.5M cap space. Solid when healthy but availability is a major concern — played only 6 games in 2025 (toe) and 8 in 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: Massive dead cap ($19.2M) if traded makes him essentially untradeable in 2026 regardless of performance. The void-year structure means cutting or trading him creates enormous cap damage. Worth a late-round pick on talent alone but contract structure prevents any trade.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $19.2M if traded due to void-year restructure makes any trade impossible. Miami locked in his 2026 salary to create cap space — they're stuck with him for the year. Will be a free agent in 2027.
players.injury_notes: Chronic injury concerns. Played only 6 games in 2025 (toe injury), 8 games in 2024, and just 2 games in 2022. This is the 3rd time in 4 seasons he played less than half the games.
players.character_notes: Described as a leader in the OL room by beat writers.

players.name: Chop Robinson
players.performance_evidence: 6.0 sacks as a rookie in 2024. Regressed to just 1.0 sack in 9 appearances in 2025 due to injuries and inconsistency. Still just 23 with elite first-step speed. Has a 5th-year option. High motor and disruption rate but sack numbers dropped significantly in year 2.

trade_value.reasoning: Young 1st-round EDGE on a rookie deal with 3 years remaining plus a 5th-year option. EDGE position premium and age (23) significantly boost value despite inconsistent 2025. A contender would pay a mid-to-late 1st for this kind of upside and contract control.

players.availability_reasoning: 1st-round pick on a cheap rookie deal is exactly the asset a rebuilding team should hold. However, new regime didn't draft him and may have different evaluation. Would take a significant offer (1st-round pick) to move him.
players.injury_notes: Suffered multiple concussions in 2025 (Weeks 9 and 17). Also had knee issues in preseason. Durability is an emerging concern.
players.trade_buzz: Beat reporters mentioned edge rushers as potential trade candidates during 2025 deadline discussions.

players.name: Zach Sieler
players.performance_evidence: Back-to-back 10-sack seasons (2023-2024) as an interior DT — extremely rare production. Led team with 10 sacks, 19 QB hits, and 13 TFL in 2024. Signed massive 3-year, $64M extension in August 2025. Called 'the heartbeat of our defense' by DC Weaver. Only IDL with 10+ sacks in consecutive seasons since 2000.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite interior pass rusher but dead cap of $30.85M makes trade impossible. If tradeable, his production would command a 1st-round pick. But the contract structure locks him in Miami.

players.availability_reasoning: $30.85M dead cap if traded makes any deal mathematically impossible for a team carrying $144M in dead money. Longest-tenured Dolphin (since 2019). Team captain and defensive cornerstone. Signed through 2029.
players.injury_notes: Remarkably durable — missed just 2 games since start of 2020 season.
players.character_notes: Team captain. Called 'heartbeat of the defense.'

players.name: Cameron Goode
players.performance_evidence: Re-signed by Dolphins in 2026 offseason. UDFA from Cal who has been a rotational edge player. Limited sack production. Depth piece elevated to DE2 after Bradley Chubb was released.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth EDGE on a minimum deal. Essentially no trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Rotational player on a minimum deal. Available for anything but unlikely to draw interest.

players.name: Jordyn Brooks
players.performance_evidence: First-team All-Pro in 2025. Led NFL with 183 tackles (99 solo). Also had 3.5 sacks, 13 TFL, 1 FF. PFF run defense grade of 92.0 (3rd among LBs). Missed tackle rate of just 4.3%. 2x AFC Defensive Player of the Week in 2025. Team captain and vocal leader.

trade_value.reasoning: All-Pro LB coming off a dominant season with 1 year remaining at $10.8M and zero dead cap if traded. Age (28) and final year status limit long-term value, but the production and low dead cap make him very attractive to contenders. Worth a 2nd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: GM Sullivan plans to extend Brooks this summer. New DC Duggan called his film 'awesome' and 'inspiring.' LB is critical in Hafley's defensive scheme. However, with no guaranteed money remaining, if extension talks stall, Brooks could become available during the season. For now, core building block.
players.injury_notes: Played all 17 games in 2025. Minor hamstring issue in Week 18 but didn't miss time. Tore ACL in 2023 but returned strong.
players.character_notes: Team captain. Praised repeatedly for leadership and culture-setting.
players.trade_buzz: ESPN's Graziano reports Dolphins plan to extend Brooks this summer. New regime views him as a building block.

players.name: Tyrel Dodson
players.performance_evidence: Former Bills UDFA who has been a solid starter alongside Brooks. Reliable tackler and capable in coverage. Not a star but a dependable LB2 who played heavy snap counts in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid starter on a reasonable 1-year deal. Zero dead cap makes him easy to move. Worth a 5th-round pick to a LB-needy contender.

players.availability_reasoning: Veteran on an expiring deal with no dead cap. Rebuilding team would move him for fair value mid-season if competing teams come calling. Not a core piece going forward.

players.name: JuJu Brents
players.performance_evidence: Former 2nd-round pick by Colts (44th overall) claimed off waivers in August 2025. 6-3, 198 lbs — long, rangy, athletic CB. Played well in limited action (14 tackles, 1 PBU, 1 FR in roughly 5 games before foot injury ended his 2025 season). Has started 9 career games across 3 seasons. Promising but injury-plagued early career.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 2nd-round pick with ideal size for an outside CB, on the final year of his rookie deal at just $1.9M. Talent is there but injury history (wrist, hamstring, knee, foot) severely limits value. Worth a 4th-5th round pick if healthy.

players.availability_reasoning: Rebuilding team claimed him for development. If he plays well in 2026, contenders could come calling mid-season. Low cost makes him easy to move.
players.injury_notes: Significant injury history: knee injury ended 2024 season (2 games), foot surgery ended 2025 season in Week 11. Also dealt with hamstring and wrist issues. Expected full recovery for 2026.

players.name: Jason Marshall Jr.
players.performance_evidence: 5th-round pick from Florida in 2025. Rookie who saw limited action in first season. Elevated to CB2 on depth chart by default after massive secondary turnover (Ramsey traded, Douglas/Kohou/Burns departed or injured).

trade_value.reasoning: Young developmental CB on a cheap rookie deal. Minimal NFL tape to evaluate. Worth a conditional late-round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Developmental piece who could be moved if Miami adds CBs through draft or free agency. Low investment.

players.name: Isaiah Johnson
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who was waived when Dolphins claimed Brents in 2025, then apparently returned to roster. Depth nickel corner. Minimal NFL production.

trade_value.reasoning: Replacement-level nickel corner. No trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Camp body at a position of need. Likely to be replaced through draft or free agency.

players.name: Dante Trader Jr.
players.performance_evidence: 5th-round pick from Maryland in 2025. Played in backup/rotational role as a rookie. Elevated to starting SS following departure of Minkah Fitzpatrick (traded to Jets) and other secondary losses.

trade_value.reasoning: Young developmental safety on a cheap rookie deal. Starting by default on a depleted roster. Worth a conditional 7th at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Starting by default, not by dominance. Miami will likely address safety in the draft, which could make Trader available.

players.name: Ashtyn Davis
players.performance_evidence: Former Jets 3rd-round pick signed by Dolphins as UFA in 2025. Journeyman safety who has bounced between starting and reserve roles. Starting FS by default after Minkah Fitzpatrick trade. Below-average starter.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran safety on a cheap 1-year deal. Zero dead cap. Could be a throw-in piece in a larger deal or moved for a late-round pick mid-season to a contender needing safety depth.

players.availability_reasoning: Placeholder starter on a rebuilding team. Would move for any draft pick. Could become a mid-season trade candidate if Miami drafts a safety or a contender has an injury.
